subject: Cloud Computing
title: IoT and the Intelligent Edge
description: A short lesson to help you apply IoT and the Intelligent Edge.

hook.text: By 2030, there will be 30 billion IoT devices. They can't all send data to the cloud. The edge must get smarter.

buildup.text: Smart sensors in factories, farms, and cities generate petabytes. Sending it all to the cloud is expensive and slow.

discovery.text: Intelligent edge devices process data locally: filter noise, detect anomalies, and send only what matters to the cloud.

twist.text: AWS IoT Greengrass, Azure IoT Edge, and Google Edge TPU bring machine learning models to tiny devices in the field.

climax.text: The future isn't cloud vs. edge. It's a seamless continuum where data flows to wherever it's processed best.

punchline.text: 30 billion devices can't all call the cloud. The edge thinks for itself.

quiz.question: Why can't IoT devices simply send all their data to the cloud?
options: The volume is too large and the latency too high for real-time decisions
IoT devices have no internet connectivity at all
Cloud providers refuse to accept data from IoT devices
